mission.title: dify: After upgrading to Dify 1.4.0, the WebScraper tool cannot be worked.
mission.description: After upgrading to Dify 1.4.0, the WebScraper tool cannot be worked.. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify dify troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your dify deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify -n dify --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review dify configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dify configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl get configmap -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dify
```
### Self Checks

### Dify version

1.4.0

### Cloud or Self Hosted

Cloud

### Steps to reproduce

After upgrading to Dify 1.40, when creating a new workflow and using the webscraper tool, this node displays the error message: "Failed to transform

steps.title: Apply the fix for After upgrading to Dify 1.4.0, the WebScraper tool cannot
steps.description:
# Summary

Replaces custom readability parsing logic with the readabilipy library for improved performance and simplicity.

Addresses improved maintainability and performance.

steps.title: Confirm After upgrading to Dify 1.4.0, the WebScraper is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify -n dify --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n dify --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
# Summary

Replaces custom readability parsing logic with the readabilipy library for improved performance and simplicity.

Addresses improved maintainability and performance.

# Screenshots

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dify installed or the issue environment reproducible.
